# Quickfynd LLM Context File (llms.txt)

Version: 2 (expanded)
Purpose: Deep orientation file for AI/code agents working in this repository.

This repository is a production e-commerce platform built on Next.js app-router APIs,
MongoDB (Mongoose), and selected PostgreSQL/Prisma integrations.

Goals of this file:
- Provide a reliable source of truth for architecture and task routing.
- Minimize hallucinations by pointing to real docs, folders, and endpoint families.
- Speed up debugging by defining where to look first for each issue class.
- Define safe handling rules for secrets, credentials, and production operations.

-----------------------------------------------------------------------
1) Executive Snapshot
-----------------------------------------------------------------------

Name: Quickfynd
Type: Full-stack e-commerce (web storefront + seller/admin + mobile support)
Framework: Next.js (app router)
Runtime: Node.js
Package manager: npm
Deployment style: Next.js build and standalone start supported

Primary business flows:
- Catalog browsing, product detail, featured/deals sections, category sliders.
- Cart/checkout with coupon, wallet, COD/prepaid logic.
- Order placement, order history, cancellation, delivery review, returns.
- Courier integrations (Delhivery + India Post + 17track event path).
- Mobile-app oriented APIs and account-linking from guest to authenticated users.
- Seller/store operations: products, orders, customers, marketing, settings.
- Messaging stack: transactional email, promotional email, WhatsApp webhook.

-----------------------------------------------------------------------
2) Runtime and Dependencies
-----------------------------------------------------------------------

Core framework/runtime:
- next, react, react-dom

Data layer:
- mongoose (MongoDB)
- prisma, @prisma/client
- @prisma/adapter-neon, @neondatabase/serverless

Payments:
- razorpay
- stripe

Auth and identity:
- firebase, firebase-admin

Media and assets:
- imagekit, imagekitio-next

Automation and async workflows:
- inngest

Email/communication:
- nodemailer
- node-mailjet
- resend
- @emailjs/nodejs

AI/search:
- @google/generative-ai
- openai

Frontend state and UI:
- @reduxjs/toolkit, react-redux
- tailwindcss, postcss

Important architectural note:
- This repo includes both MongoDB-first application data and Prisma/Postgres setup.
- Many operational and domain docs are maintained as top-level markdown guides.

-----------------------------------------------------------------------
3) Commands and Operational Entry Points
-----------------------------------------------------------------------

Install:
- npm install

Development:
- npm run dev
- npm run dev:turbo

Build and serve:
- npm run build
- npm run start
- npm run start:standalone
- npm run start:legacy

Quality check:
- npm run lint

Identity migration:
- npm run migrate:user-identity-indexes
- npm run migrate:user-identity-indexes:apply

Useful scripts folder commands (run via node):
- scripts/migrateUserIdentityIndexes.js
- scripts/migrateOrderAddresses.js
- scripts/verify-security.js
- scripts/fixProductSlugs.js
- scripts/fixProductImages.js
- scripts/fixStoreAndProducts.js
- scripts/removeBrokenProductImages.js
- scripts/updateImageKitUrls.js
- scripts/checkRecentOrders.js
- scripts/checkOrder.js
- scripts/checkAddresses.js
- scripts/createTestOrders.js

-----------------------------------------------------------------------
4) High-Priority Docs to Read First
-----------------------------------------------------------------------

General onboarding path:
1. README.md
2. IMPLEMENTATION_COMPLETE_INDEX.md
3. BACKEND_DOCUMENTATION_INDEX.md
4. API_QUICK_REFERENCE.md
5. DEPLOYMENT_CHECKLIST.md

Mobile app integration path:
1. MOBILE_APP_DEVELOPER_QUICKSTART.md
2. MOBILE_API_HANDOFF.md
3. MOBILE_AUTH_PROFILE_ORDER_TRACKING_IMPLEMENTATION_GUIDE.md
4. CHECKOUT_WALLET_COUPON_OFFERS_COMPLETE_GUIDE.md

Production deployment path:
1. DEPLOYMENT_CHECKLIST.md
2. BACKEND_DEPLOYMENT_CHECKLIST.md
3. BACKEND_ACCOUNT_LINKING_DEPLOYMENT.md
4. SECURITY_CHECKLIST.md

-----------------------------------------------------------------------
5) Repository Layout and Responsibilities
-----------------------------------------------------------------------

- app/
	Next.js routes/pages and API route handlers.
- app/api/
	Main backend API surface (app-router handlers).
- components/
	Reusable UI components for storefront/admin screens.
- lib/
	Shared service wrappers (shipping, payment, email, auth, db helpers).
- models/
	Mongoose schemas and domain entities.
- prisma/
	Prisma schema and postgres-related data assets.
- scripts/
	One-off and repeatable maintenance/migration scripts.
- middlewares/ and middleware.ts
	Request pipeline/auth/security handling.
- configs/
	Config modules and feature config data.
- inngest/
	Event/workflow orchestration code.
- public/
	Publicly served assets and static files (including public/llms.txt).

-----------------------------------------------------------------------
6) API Surface Map (Grouped)
-----------------------------------------------------------------------

The repository contains a large API surface under app/api (200+ route handlers).
Below is a grouped map of the main domains and representative routes.

A) Auth, profile, identity, guest linking
- app/api/profile/route.js
- app/api/user/profile/route.js
- app/api/user/link-identities/route.js
- app/api/user/link-guest-orders/route.js
- app/api/guest/convert-account/route.js
- app/api/auth/otp/send/route.js
- app/api/auth/otp/verify/route.js
- app/api/account/delete/route.js

B) Catalog, products, search, browse
- app/api/products/route.js
- app/api/products/by-slug/route.js
- app/api/products/batch/route.js
- app/api/products/top-rated/route.js
- app/api/products/deals/route.js
- app/api/products/[id]/fbt/route.js
- app/api/categories/route.js
- app/api/search-products/route.js
- app/api/search-by-image/route.js
- app/api/browse-history/route.js
- app/api/customer/recent-searches/route.js

C) Cart, checkout, pricing, coupon, wallet
- app/api/cart/route.js
- app/api/cart/validate/route.js
- app/api/coupon/route.js
- app/api/coupons/route.js
- app/api/wallet/route.js
- app/api/wallet/bonus/route.js
- app/api/shipping/route.js
- app/api/store/checkout/route.js

D) Orders and post-order flows
- app/api/orders/route.js
- app/api/orders/cancel/route.js
- app/api/orders/failed/route.js
- app/api/orders/delivery-review/route.js
- app/api/orders/return-request/route.js
- app/api/orders/check-razorpay-settlement/route.js
- app/api/return-request/route.js
- app/api/track-order/route.js

E) Payments and webhooks
- app/api/razorpay/order/route.js
- app/api/razorpay/verify/route.js
- app/api/razorpay/webhook/route.js
- app/api/webhooks/razorpay/route.js
- app/api/stripe/route.js

F) Courier and logistics
- app/api/delhivery/waybill/route.js
- app/api/delhivery/create-shipment/route.js
- app/api/delhivery/track/route.js
- app/api/delhivery/shipping-cost/route.js
- app/api/delhivery/pincode-serviceability/route.js
- app/api/delhivery/pickup-request/route.js
- app/api/delhivery/label/route.js
- app/api/indiapost/pincode/route.js
- app/api/indiapost/booking/route.js
- app/api/indiapost/tracking/bulk/route.js
- app/api/indiapost/events/route.js
- app/api/india-post-tracking/route.js
- app/api/17track/webhook/route.js

G) Store/seller/admin domain (selected)
- app/api/store/profile/update/route.js
- app/api/store/settings/route.js
- app/api/store/products/pricing/route.js
- app/api/store/orders/route.js
- app/api/store/orders/[orderId]/route.js
- app/api/store/orders/update-status/route.js
- app/api/store/return-requests/route.js
- app/api/store/customers/route.js
- app/api/store/customers/wallet/route.js
- app/api/store/sales-report/route.js
- app/api/store/sales-report/export/route.js
- app/api/store/coupons/route.js
- app/api/store/coupon/[code]/route.js
- app/api/store/deals/route.js
- app/api/store/meta-integration/route.js
- app/api/store/marketing-expenses/route.js
- app/api/store/spin-campaign/route.js
- app/api/store/referral-program/route.js
- app/api/admin/dashboard/route.js
- app/api/admin/categories/route.js
- app/api/admin/stores/route.js

H) Content/home/experience configuration
- app/api/home/sections/route.js
- app/api/home-selection/route.js
- app/api/public/featured-sections/route.js
- app/api/public/category-sliders/route.js
- app/api/public/most-selling-products/route.js
- app/api/store/hero-slider/route.js
- app/api/store/home-categories/route.js
- app/api/store/category-slider/route.js
- app/api/store/category-menu/route.js

I) Notifications, engagement, and support
- app/api/notifications/order-status/route.js
- app/api/notifications/guest-order/route.js
- app/api/send-welcome-email/route.js
- app/api/send-login-email/route.js
- app/api/send-signout-email/route.js
- app/api/send-shipping-email/route.js
- app/api/promotional-emails/route.js
- app/api/promotional-emails/templates/route.js
- app/api/whatsapp-test/route.js
- app/api/tickets/route.js
- app/api/tickets/[ticketId]/route.js
- app/api/store/tickets/route.js

J) Analytics/experiments/debug/test helpers
- app/api/analytics/customer-behavior/route.js
- app/api/analytics/track-attribution/route.js
- app/api/debug/mongodb/route.js
- app/api/debug/sliders/route.js
- app/api/test/setup/route.js
- app/api/test/create-sample-coupon/route.js

- models/User.js

- models/Category.js
- models/Order.js

- models/BrowseHistory.js
- models/RecentSearch.js
- models/PersonalizedOffer.js

Store/seller operations:
- models/Store.js
- models/StoreUser.js
- models/StoreMenu.js
- models/StoreNotificationSetting.js
- models/SitemapSettings.js
- models/ShippingSetting.js
- models/MarketingExpense.js
- models/MetaIntegration.js

- models/FeaturedSection.js
- models/GridSection.js
- models/HomeSection.js
- models/HomeCategorySettings.js
- models/HomeMenuCategorySettings.js

- models/SpinCampaign.js
- models/SpinLog.js

- models/ReferralProgramSettings.js
- models/CustomerReferral.js

- models/Ticket.js
- models/ReturnRequest.js
- models/OtpChallenge.js
- models/Waybill.js
- models/EmailTemplate.js
- models/EmailHistory.js

Note:
- If a route references a model not listed above, inspect route imports first before adding docs.

- lib/cache.js

- lib/otp.js

-----------------------------------------------------------------------
10) Environment Variable Contract (Names Only)
-----------------------------------------------------------------------

Rules:
- Never output actual secret values in logs/docs/responses.
- Use placeholders in examples.
- Rotate any credential that may have been exposed outside secure channels.

Expected variable groups in this project:

App/runtime/public:
- NEXT_PUBLIC_BASE_URL
- NEXT_PUBLIC_APP_URL
- NEXT_PUBLIC_CURRENCY_SYMBOL
- NEXT_PUBLIC_ADMIN_EMAIL

Database:
- MONGODB_URI
- DATABASE_URL
- DIRECT_URL

Firebase/auth:
- NEXT_PUBLIC_FIREBASE_API_KEY
- NEXT_PUBLIC_FIREBASE_AUTH_DOMAIN
- NEXT_PUBLIC_FIREBASE_PROJECT_ID
- NEXT_PUBLIC_FIREBASE_STORAGE_BUCKET
- NEXT_PUBLIC_FIREBASE_MESSAGING_SENDER_ID
- NEXT_PUBLIC_FIREBASE_APP_ID
- NEXT_PUBLIC_FIREBASE_MEASUREMENT_ID
- FIREBASE_SERVICE_ACCOUNT_KEY
- NEXT_PUBLIC_GOOGLE_ONE_TAP_CLIENT_ID

Payment:
- NEXT_PUBLIC_RAZORPAY_KEY_ID
- RAZORPAY_KEY_SECRET
- RAZORPAY_MERCHANT_ID
- STRIPE_SECRET_KEY
- STRIPE_WEBHOOK_SECRET

Shipping/courier/tracking:
- DELHIVERY_API_TOKEN
- DELHIVERY_LTL_USERNAME
- DELHIVERY_LTL_PASSWORD
- DELHIVERY_LTL_BASE_URL
- SEVENTEENTRACK_API_KEY

Media/storage:
- IMAGEKIT_PUBLIC_KEY
- IMAGEKIT_PRIVATE_KEY
- IMAGEKIT_URL_ENDPOINT
- NEXT_PUBLIC_IMAGEKIT_PUBLIC_KEY
- NEXT_PUBLIC_IMAGEKIT_URL_ENDPOINT

Email and messaging:
- SMTP_HOST
- SMTP_PORT
- SMTP_USER
- SMTP_PASS
- MAILJET_API_KEY
- MAILJET_SECRET_KEY
- RESEND_API_KEY
- EMAIL_FROM
- EMAIL_FROM_TRANSACTIONAL
- EMAIL_FROM_MARKETING
- WHATSPORTAL_WEBHOOK_URL
- WHATSPORTAL_WEBHOOK_TOKEN

AI and automation:
- GEMINI_API_KEY
- OPENAI_BASE_URL
- OPENAI_MODEL
- INNGEST_EVENT_KEY
- INNGEST_SIGNING_KEY

-----------------------------------------------------------------------
11) Task-Oriented Playbooks for LLM Agents
-----------------------------------------------------------------------

If task is cart or checkout bug:
- Read CART_INDEX.md, CART_IMPLEMENTATION_GUIDE.md, CHECKOUT_FIXES.md.
- Inspect app/api/cart/* and app/api/store/checkout/route.js.
- Validate coupon/wallet interplay via app/api/coupon*, app/api/wallet*.

If task is mobile app API mismatch:
- Read MOBILE_APP_DEVELOPER_QUICKSTART.md and MOBILE_API_HANDOFF.md.
- Check profile/account-linking routes first.
- Compare expected headers/source markers before payload-level debugging.

If task is order placement or payment failure:
- Inspect app/api/orders/route.js and razorpay/stripe routes.
- Verify webhook route behavior and signature handling.
- Cross-check COD/prepaid conversion docs and settlement guides.

If task is customer returns, replacements, or refunds:
- Read CUSTOMER_RETURNS_GUIDE.md (comprehensive end-to-end).
- Inspect app/api/return-request/route.js and app/api/orders/delivery-review/route.js.
- Check models/ReturnRequest.js and Order.returns sub-schema.
- Verify seller return approval flow in app/api/store/return-requests/route.js.

If task is courier/tracking issue:
- Start with app/api/track-order/route.js and delhivery/indiapost routes.
- Confirm AWB generation and pickup request routes.
- Read DELHIVERY_* and INDIA_POST_* guides for expected status transitions.

If task is store/admin dashboard issue:
- Focus on app/api/store/* and app/api/admin/* family.
- Inspect model relations in Store, StoreUser, Product, Order.
- Verify permissions and role assumptions in route-level auth.

If task is email/notification issue:
- Inspect lib/nodemailer.js, lib/emailService.js, template/history models.
- Verify route-specific triggers in send-* endpoints.
- Check provider-specific setup docs before code changes.

-----------------------------------------------------------------------
12) Safety, Security, and Production Change Rules
-----------------------------------------------------------------------

Non-negotiables for agent behavior:
- Never print credentials, keys, tokens, private keys, or full connection strings.
- Never commit raw secrets into tracked files.
- Prefer .env placeholders in documentation snippets.
- For security-sensitive fixes, propose rotation and invalidation steps.
- For migrations, run dry-run or verification scripts before apply.

Production-safe workflow:
1. Read relevant domain docs.
2. Locate exact route/model/lib touched.
3. Implement narrow change.
4. Run lint/build or targeted checks.
5. Verify no secret leakage in logs/diffs.
6. Update docs and this llms.txt if behavior changed.

-----------------------------------------------------------------------
13) Known Cross-Cutting Concerns
-----------------------------------------------------------------------

- Hybrid data strategy (Mongo + Prisma/Postgres) requires care in assumptions.
- Guest-to-user linking and identity normalization impacts profile/order visibility.
- Checkout stack combines coupons, wallet, COD rules, and shipping decisions.
- Multi-provider communications stack means fallback paths can diverge.
- Route count is high; always identify exact handler path before changing logic.

-----------------------------------------------------------------------
14) Maintenance Policy for this File
-----------------------------------------------------------------------

Update this file whenever:
- New major API families are introduced.
- Data model structure changes materially.
- New third-party integrations are added or replaced.
- Deployment scripts or migration process changes.
- Documentation entry points are renamed.

Recommended PR rule:
- Any significant architecture or API change should include llms.txt updates.

Public availability:
- Keep root llms.txt and public/llms.txt in sync.
